title: pedicularis integrifolia subsp. integerrima
intro: Pedicularis integrifolia subsp. integerrima, also known as Lousewort, is a flowering plant species in the bumblebee family (Orobanchaceae), native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Pedicularis integrifolia subsp. integerrima have been attributed to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, colds, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has been used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
